messages.content:
I want to start a campaing based in a city like neverwinter. I will play a divination wizard, half-elf.

Age: 34 (Appears in his late 20s by human standards, but possesses a weary, old soul energy)

Appearance: Lucan has the striking, slightly angular features of his elven mother, paired with the broader shoulders and scruffy jawline of his human father. He has messy, silver-streaked dark hair that he constantly forgets to brush, and striking, heterochromic eyes—one a piercing sea-green, the other a cloudy, pale blue (his "sight" eye). He dresses in practical, ink-stained traveling robes, favoring deep blues and tattered silver trim. He is rarely seen without a deck of worn, hand-painted tarokka cards or a smooth obsidian orb.

Personality: Calm, slightly detached, and possessed of a dry, cynical wit. Because he frequently experiences flashes of the immediate future, he is rarely surprised, which others often mistake for arrogance or boredom. He has a habit of answering questions a split second before they are asked.

The Backstory: The Burden of the Second GlanceLucan was born in the bustling cosmopolitan city of Neverwinter, the product of a brief romance between a human cartographer and an elven chronomancer. Growing up, Lucan was plagued by severe migraines and vivid, terrifying nightmares. It wasn't until his teenage years that his family realized these weren't nightmares—they were ripples of timelines that hadn't happened yet.Recognizing his raw, uncontrolled talent, his mother secured him an apprenticeship at a prestigious magical academy. While other wizards spent decades memorizing complex evocation formulas to summon fire and lightning, Lucan focused entirely on the delicate, maddening art of Divination. He learned to unravel the threads of time, peer through the veil of reality, and manipulate probability itself ($Portent$).However, seeing the future is a heavy burden. In his late 20s, Lucan foresaw a terrible magical mishap at the academy that would cost the lives of several students. He tried to warn the Headmaster, but his warnings were dismissed as the paranoia of a young seer. Lucan took matters into his own hands, discreetly altering the arcane components of the experiment. He prevented the disaster, but the academy caught him tampering with the materials and expelled him for "reckless endangerment and sabotage." Unable to prove a catastrophe that never happened, Lucan packed his spellbook and left.Current MotivationNow in his mid-30s, Lucan lives as a seer and investigator for hire. He wanders from town to town, using his magic to find missing persons, predict treacherous weather for merchant caravans, and occasionally cheat at dice when funds run low. He is haunted by a specific, recurring vision he had a year ago: a encroaching shadow swallowing the realm, and a specific group of adventurers (the campaign's party) standing in the center of it. He seeks them out not out of heroism, but because he knows that if he doesn't guide them to the right path, there won't be a future left to foresee.

I want to start a campaing based in a city like neverwinter. I will play a divination wizard, half-elf.

Age: 34 (Appears in his late 20s by human standards, but possesses a weary, old soul energy)

Appearance: Lucan has the striking, slightly angular features of his elven mother, paired with the broader shoulders and scruffy jawline of his human father. He has messy, silver-streaked dark hair that he constantly forgets to brush, and striking, heterochromic eyes—one a piercing sea-green, the other a cloudy, pale blue (his "sight" eye). He dresses in practical, ink-stained traveling robes, favoring deep blues and tattered silver trim. He is rarely seen without a deck of worn, hand-painted tarokka cards or a smooth obsidian orb.

Personality: Calm, slightly detached, and possessed of a dry, cynical wit. Because he frequently experiences flashes of the immediate future, he is rarely surprised, which others often mistake for arrogance or boredom. He has a habit of answering questions a split second before they are asked.

The Backstory: The Burden of the Second GlanceLucan was born in the bustling cosmopolitan city of Neverwinter, the product of a brief romance between a human cartographer and an elven chronomancer. Growing up, Lucan was plagued by severe migraines and vivid, terrifying nightmares. It wasn't until his teenage years that his family realized these weren't nightmares—they were ripples of timelines that hadn't happened yet.Recognizing his raw, uncontrolled talent, his mother secured him an apprenticeship at a prestigious magical academy. While other wizards spent decades memorizing complex evocation formulas to summon fire and lightning, Lucan focused entirely on the delicate, maddening art of Divination. He learned to unravel the threads of time, peer through the veil of reality, and manipulate probability itself ($Portent$).However, seeing the future is a heavy burden. In his late 20s, Lucan foresaw a terrible magical mishap at the academy that would cost the lives of several students. He tried to warn the Headmaster, but his warnings were dismissed as the paranoia of a young seer. Lucan took matters into his own hands, discreetly altering the arcane components of the experiment. He prevented the disaster, but the academy caught him tampering with the materials and expelled him for "reckless endangerment and sabotage." Unable to prove a catastrophe that never happened, Lucan packed his spellbook and left.Current MotivationNow in his mid-30s, Lucan lives as a seer and investigator for hire. He wanders from town to town, using his magic to find missing persons, predict treacherous weather for merchant caravans, and occasionally cheat at dice when funds run low. He is haunted by a specific, recurring vision he had a year ago: a encroaching shadow swallowing the realm, and a specific group of adventurers (the campaign's party) standing in the center of it. He seeks them out not out of heroism, but because he knows that if he doesn't guide them to the right path, there won't be a future left to foresee.

Welcome, adventurer. Before we begin — what kind of world do you want to enter?

1. **Classic High Fantasy** — Ancient kingdoms, dragon-haunted mountains, magic woven into the fabric of reality.
2. **Dark Gothic** — Crumbling castles, cursed bloodlines, horrors that wear human faces.
3. **Seafaring** — Uncharted oceans, buried treasure, storms that swallow ships whole.
4. **Political Intrigue** — Courts of power, poisoned alliances, secrets that topple dynasties.

Which calls to you?

Now let's choose your class. Here are all 12 options:

1. **Barbarian** (1d12) — Rage-fueled warrior. Hits the hardest, tanks the most.
2. **Bard** (1d8 [Charisma spellcaster]) — Performer-mage. Spells, skills, and silver tongue.
3. **Cleric** (1d8 [Wisdom spellcaster]) — Divine conduit. Healer or warrior depending on your god.
4. **Druid** (1d8 [Wisdom spellcaster]) — Nature's voice. Shapeshifts, storms, and wild magic.
5. **Fighter** (1d10) — Martial master. Every weapon, any battlefield.
6. **Monk** (1d8) — Living weapon. Speed and precision, no armor needed.
7. **Paladin** (1d10 [Charisma spellcaster]) — Sacred oath warrior. Heavy armor, divine spells, aura of protection.
8. **Ranger** (1d10 [Wisdom spellcaster]) — Wilderness hunter. Tracking, archery, beast companion.
9. **Rogue** (1d8) — Shadow striker. Sneak attacks, skills, and quick escapes.
10. **Sorcerer** (1d6 [Charisma spellcaster]) — Born with magic in the blood. Raw power, limited spells.
11. **Warlock** (1d8 [Charisma spellcaster]) — Pact-bound spellcaster. Few strong spells, recover on short rest.
12. **Wizard** (1d6 [Intelligence spellcaster]) — Studied mage. Largest spell list, most versatile magic.

Which class calls to you?

Wizard — now choose your archetype.
*(This archetype unlocks at level 3, but defines your character's path from the start.)*

• **School of Abjuration** — The School of Abjuration emphasizes magic that blocks, banishes, or protects.
• **School of Conjuration** — As a Conjurer, you favor spells that produce objects and creatures out of thin air.
• **School of Divination** — The counsel of a Diviner is sought by royalty and commoners alike, for all seek a clearer understanding of the past, present, and future.
• **School of Enchantment** — As a member of the School of Enchantment, you have honed your ability to magically entrance and beguile other people and monsters.
• **School of Evocation** — You focus your study on magic that creates powerful elemental effects such as bitter cold, searing flame, rolling thunder, crackling lightning, and burning acid.
• **School of Illusion** — You focus your studies on magic that dazzles the senses, befuddles the mind, and tricks even the wisest folk.
• **School of Necromancy** — The School of Necromancy explores the cosmic forces of life, death, and undeath.
• **School of Transmutation** — You are a student of spells that modify energy and matter.

Which path calls to you?
divination

Now choose your race:

1. **Human** — +1 all stats (or Variant: +1 two stats, bonus skill, feat).
2. **Elf** — +2 DEX, darkvision, Fey Ancestry, Trance.
3. **Dwarf** — +2 CON, darkvision, poison resistance, stonecunning.
4. **Halfling** — +2 DEX, Halfling Luck (reroll nat 1s), Brave.
5. **Half-Elf** — +2 CHA, +1 to two stats, darkvision, two free skills.
6. **Half-Orc** — +2 STR, +1 CON, darkvision, Relentless Endurance, Savage Attacks.
7. **Gnome** — +2 INT, darkvision, advantage on INT/WIS/CHA magic saves.
8. **Tiefling** — +2 CHA, +1 INT, darkvision, fire resistance, innate spells.
9. **Dragonborn** — +2 STR, +1 CHA, breath weapon, damage resistance.

Which race are you?

Choose your background:

1. **Acolyte** — Temple servant. +Insight, Religion.
2. **Charlatan** — Con artist. +Deception, Sleight of Hand.
3. **Criminal** — Outlaw. +Deception, Stealth.
4. **Entertainer** — Performer. +Acrobatics, Performance.
5. **Folk Hero** — Common champion. +Animal Handling, Survival.
6. **Hermit** — Recluse. +Medicine, Religion.
7. **Noble** — Privileged. +History, Persuasion.
8. **Outlander** — Wilderness wanderer. +Athletics, Survival.
9. **Sage** — Scholar. +Arcana, History.
10. **Sailor** — Sea veteran. +Athletics, Perception.
11. **Soldier** — Military. +Athletics, Intimidation.
12. **Urchin** — Street kid. +Sleight of Hand, Stealth.

Which fits your past?

Now choose your skill proficiencies.

As a **Wizard**, pick **2** from:

• ~~Arcana~~ *(background)*
• ~~History~~ *(background)*
• Insight
• Investigation
• Medicine
• Religion

*Your **Sage** background already grants: Arcana, History.*

Which 2 do you want?
Insight and Investigation

Rolling your stats — 4d6, drop lowest, for each ability score.

**STR:** [3,5,6,6] → drop 3 = **17**
**DEX:** [1,1,5,5] → drop 1 = **11**
**CON:** [2,2,4,4] → drop 2 = **10**
**INT:** [1,4,4,4] → drop 1 = **12**
**WIS:** [3,4,4,4] → drop 3 = **12**
**CHA:** [2,3,4,5] → drop 2 = **12**

**Suggested assignment for Wizard:** INT → DEX → CON → WIS → CHA → STR

You have: **17, 12, 12, 12, 11, 10**. How would you like to assign these to STR / DEX / CON / INT / WIS / CHA?
use the suggested assignment, making STR the lowest

**Starting Spells for Wizard**
Wizards learn spells in their spellbook. You start with 6 level-1 spells plus Mage Armor and Magic Missile (already in your book).

**Cantrips — choose 3:**
1. Fire Bolt
2. Ray of Frost
3. Shocking Grasp
4. Chill Touch
5. Mage Hand
6. Minor Illusion
7. Prestidigitation
8. Poison Spray
9. True Strike
10. Light
11. Acid Splash
12. Message
13. Dancing Lights
14. Blade Ward

**Level 1 Spells — choose 6:**
1. Sleep
2. Burning Hands
3. Charm Person
4. Color Spray
5. Comprehend Languages
6. Detect Magic
7. Disguise Self
8. Expeditious Retreat
9. False Life
10. Feather Fall
11. Find Familiar
12. Fog Cloud
13. Grease
14. Identify
15. Jump
16. Longstrider
17. Shield
18. Silent Image
19. Thunderwave
20. Witch Bolt

Tell me which you want — e.g. "Cantrips: Vicious Mockery, Minor Illusion / Spells: Cure Wounds, Healing Word, Sleep, Faerie Fire"

Cantrips: Fire bolt, mage hand and minor illusion
level 1 spells: sleep, detect magic, find familiar, shield, thunderwave, fog cloud

Here is your starting equipment:

**From your Wizard class:**
- Quarterstaff
- Spellbook (6 chosen spells + Mage Armor + Magic Missile)
- Scholar's pack
- Arcane focus
- Component pouch

**From your Sage background:**
- Ink bottle
- Ink pen
- Small knife
- Letter from a dead colleague
- Common clothes
- Belt pouch (10 gp)

Ready to name your character?
yes

Almost done — let's bring your character to life.

**1. What is your character's name?**

**2. What does your Half-Elf Wizard look like?** A scar, a striking feature, something that sets you apart.

**3. (Optional but encouraged)** Who are they? Where do they come from? What drives them — a loss, a debt, a promise?

*Share whatever feels right. This becomes the DM's bible for your character.*

Growing up, Lucan was plagued by severe migraines and vivid, terrifying nightmares. It wasn't until his teenage years that his family realized these weren't nightmares—they were ripples of timelines that hadn't happened yet.Recognizing his raw, uncontrolled talent, his mother secured him an apprenticeship at a prestigious magical academy. While other wizards spent decades memorizing complex evocation formulas to summon fire and lightning, Lucan focused entirely on the delicate, maddening art of Divination. He learned to unravel the threads of time, peer through the veil of reality, and manipulate probability itself ($Portent$).However, seeing the future is a heavy burden. In his late 20s, Lucan foresaw a terrible magical mishap at the academy that would cost the lives of several students. He tried to warn the Headmaster, but his warnings were dismissed as the paranoia of a young seer. Lucan took matters into his own hands, discreetly altering the arcane components of the experiment. He prevented the disaster, but the academy caught him tampering with the materials and expelled him for "reckless endangerment and sabotage." Unable to prove a catastrophe that never happened, Lucan packed his spellbook and left.Current MotivationNow in his mid-30s, Lucan lives as a mercenary seer and investigator for hire. He wanders from town to town, using his magic to find missing persons, predict treacherous weather for merchant caravans, and occasionally cheat at dice when funds run low. He is haunted by a specific, recurring vision he had a year ago: a encroaching shadow swallowing the realm, and a specific group of adventurers (the campaign's party) standing in the center of it. He seeks them out not out of heroism, but because he knows that if he doesn't guide them to the right path, there won't be a future left to foresee.

Lucan is a weary soul of thirty-four, bearing the angular grace of his elven heritage softened by the rugged, scruffy build of his human father, all topped with a crown of perpetually unkempt, silver-streaked dark hair. His most arresting feature is his heterochromia; his sea-green eye reflects the present, while his cloudy, pale blue "sight" eye constantly flickers toward the shifting threads of the future. Clad in practical, ink-stained robes of deep blue and silver, he is never without his worn tarokka cards or a smooth obsidian orb, artifacts of a man who has traded his youth for the burden of foresight. Cast out from a prestigious academy for sabotaging an experiment to prevent a disaster only he could see, he now drifts as a cynical, detached investigator for hire. Haunted by a recurring vision of an encroaching shadow, Lucan tracks the party not out of altruism, but from a desperate, calculated necessity to ensure their survival, for he knows their fates are inextricably woven into the future of the realm.

✅ **Character sheet complete!**

**Lucan** — Wizard, Level 1 Half-Elf
**HP:** 7 | **AC:** 12 (with Mage Armor)
**Skills:** Insight, Investigation, Arcana, History
**Background:** Sage

*Your adventure begins...*
Hi

The morning air in the frontier town of Oakhaven is crisp, smelling of pine needles, wet earth, and the faint, acrid tang of woodsmoke from the nearby hearths. You stand near the edge of the town square, the cobblestones slick with a light, lingering dew. 

Your cloudy, pale blue eye twitches, the familiar, nauseating sensation of a splintering timeline brushing against your consciousness. You blink, the vision of a tavern door swinging open—a man in a blood-stained cloak stumbling out—flashing before you in a blur of grey, then snapping back to the mundane reality of a merchant cart rattling past, its wheels groaning under the weight of crates.

A few yards away, a woman with a heavy, fur-lined cloak stands near the town notice board, her fingers tracing the edge of a parchment. She hasn't turned around yet, but you already know the slight hitch in her breath—a nervous tic she’ll develop in about ten seconds when the town crier starts shouting the morning news.

The mundane world moves forward, but you are already steps ahead of it. What do you do?
I approach the woman with the fur-lined cloak

You step across the slick cobblestones, your boots making soft, rhythmic thuds against the wet stone. As you draw within a few paces of the woman, the town crier—a rotund man with a face like a bruised plum—steps onto a wooden crate nearby and lets out a brassy, jarring shout about the rising price of grain. 

Just as the sound rips through the morning quiet, the woman’s shoulders jump, and she lets out that sharp, uneven hitch of breath you foresaw. She clutches her cloak tighter, her knuckles white, and spins around to face you, startled. Her eyes are wide, darting toward the notice board and then back to your face. She looks as though she’s been caught doing something she shouldn't, or perhaps, as though she’s been waiting for someone to finally notice her.

"I—I'm sorry," she stammers, her voice brittle. She glances at your ink-stained robes, then at the cloudy, pale blue of your eye, and she seems to shrink slightly into her furs. "I didn't mean to… were you looking for the notice, too? Or are you just another traveler passing through this gods-forsaken mud-pit?" 

She gestures vaguely toward the parchment on the board, which appears to be a crudely drawn map with a heavy red "X" marked over a location in the nearby Whispering Woods.

Lucan says: "Aye, I was just going to read the notice board, search for something new. I see you already marked something (looking at what she is pointing). Didn't mean to startle you, but I foresaw you jumping in surprise of the town's crier morning news. My name is Lucan, wizard. You are?"

The woman stares at you, her mouth slightly agape. The color drains from her cheeks, leaving her looking as pale as the morning mist clinging to the square. She doesn't seem to have heard your name; she is entirely preoccupied by your casual mention of the town crier's outburst.

"You... you knew?" she whispers, her gaze dropping from your sea-green eye to that unsettling, milky-blue orb of your 'sight' eye. She shivers, and not just from the damp morning chill. "I didn't think anyone actually *felt* it. I thought it was just me."

She pulls her cloak even tighter, as if trying to hide the very shape of her body from your vision. "I'm Elara," she says, her voice regaining a fraction of its strength, though she remains wary. She glances back at the notice board, the red 'X' on the map seeming to pulse in the dim light. "I didn't mark that map, traveler. It was there when I arrived this morning. I was just... checking to see if anyone else had signed their name below it."

She gestures to the bottom of the parchment. Where there should be a list of volunteers or interested parties, there is only a single, jagged smear of black ink—a symbol that looks disturbingly like a weeping eye. 

"I'm not a wizard," she adds, her eyes searching your face for any sign of deception. "I'm just a scout. And if you're looking for something 'new' in this place, you'd be better off looking for a way out before the sun sets. The woods aren't just whispering anymore, Lucan. They’re screaming."

She takes a half-step back, her hand resting instinctively on the hilt of a short sword hidden beneath her cloak. "Why are you really here? You don't look like the type to hunt for grain prices."

Lucan says: "Oh yeah, I know you didn't mark the X. You where just pointing at it I know. I don't know, something just told me I had to be here at this exact moment and time, Elara. Maybe you have something to offer me, or maybe it was just meant for us to meet at this morning. Sometimes fate works like this. (Lucan takes a look at the parchment with the X marked on it). Whats at this whispering woods?"

*Lucan, you lean in, your sea-green eye tracking the texture of the parchment while your "sight" eye drifts, unfocused, catching the faint, shimmering residue of a transmutation spell lingering on the paper. It wasn't drawn with charcoal; it was burned into the fiber with something far more volatile.*

"Fate," Elara echoes, the word tasting like ash in her mouth. She doesn't seem comforted by the concept. She watches you study the map, her posture tight. "If fate brought you here, then fate has a cruel sense of humor."

As you focus on the red 'X' and the weeping eye symbol, your mind brushes against the threads of what lies in the Whispering Woods. It’s not just a location; it's a convergence. You feel a rhythmic, low-frequency pulse—like a heartbeat buried deep under the roots of ancient trees. The symbol isn't a warning; it’s a beacon.

"That," Elara says, nodding toward the 'X' as if she can sense the weight of your focus, "is the site of the old ranger outpost. Or it was, until three days ago. The scouts who went in to check on the trade route didn't come back. But their horses did. They came back wandering the perimeter of the woods, covered in frost, despite the summer heat."

She leans in, her voice dropping to a harsh, jagged whisper. "The villagers think it's bandits or wolves. But I've been tracking the prints in the mud near the treeline. They aren't human, and they aren't beasts. They’re... hollow. Whatever is at that 'X' has stripped the life out of the woods. The birds have gone silent. Even the wind feels like it's holding its breath."

She looks at your obsidian orb, then back to your eyes, her expression shifting from fear to a desperate, calculating curiosity. "You talk about fate like it's a map. If you're as good as you seem to think you are, tell me: does the path lead into the trees, or does it end at the edge of a grave?"

update_location("Oakhaven Town Square")
add_quest("The Whispering Woods", "Investigate the marked ranger outpost and the source of the silence.")

There are something you can't ever foretell, even the most proficient divination wizards and seers can't. 
1. The time of your death
2. whether cats are actually sentient or they're just messing with ya. (Lucan laughs).

I cannot tell you if fate will bring you to the edge of a grave, but I can offer my help with whatever is brooding inside that forest? whatcha say Elara? (As Lucan says the name Elara, it becomes etched in the sleeve of his robes, not noticeable)

Elara stares at you, the dark, humorless set of her mouth twitching, caught between genuine annoyance and a reluctant, startled smile. The laugh you let out seems to deflate the tension in her shoulders, though the hollow look in her eyes remains.

"Cats," she mutters, shaking her head. "You're a strange one, Lucan. Most people who claim to see the future are either trying to sell me a prophecy for a gold piece or are raving mad in the gutters."

She looks toward the edge of the town, where the tree line of the Whispering Woods looms like a wall of iron-grey shadows, oppressive even in the mid-morning sun. She draws a sharp, steadying breath, her hand finally falling away from her sword hilt. As she does, the fabric of your sleeve ripples—a faint, silver glimmer of arcane energy briefly illuminating the name *Elara* as it binds itself into the fibers of your robe, a silent, unseen mark of a connection forged in the threads of time.

"If you're willing to walk into that silence," she says, her voice low and firm, "I won't turn you away. I've been waiting for someone who doesn't look at that map and see only danger. I see an opportunity to stop whatever is draining the life out of this place before it reaches the town walls."

She turns away from the board, adjusting her cloak. "I'm heading to the livery to grab my gear. If you’re coming, don't take too long. The sun is climbing, and I'd rather not be deep in those trees once the light starts to fail. We meet at the North Gate in ten minutes. And Lucan?" 

She pauses, glancing back at you with a piercing, appraising look. "If you see me fall... do me a favor and don't tell me about it beforehand. I'd like to keep at least that much of my future a surprise."

With that, she pivots and strides briskly toward the stables, leaving you standing before the smoldering, weeping eye on the parchment.

*What would you like to do before the ten minutes are up?*
